name: Morentin-Dicastillo por el río Ega – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aberin features a diverse landscape of valley hillsides and varied terrain, offering a mix of ascents and descents. The area includes trails along the Río Ega and routes near features like the Muro de Aberin, providing different riding experiences. Mountain bikers can find paths that traverse natural landscapes, from riverbanks to higher ground. Best mountain bike trails around Aberin The most popular mountain bike trail is Morentin-Dicastillo por el río Ega – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT), a 14.4 miles (23.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Ega river, offering scenic views and a mix of riverside paths and climbs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Muro de Aberin. Robert Innova – Saint John the Baptist loop from Villatuerta, a moderate 27.2 miles (43.7 km) path. This route includes sections around the Muro de Aberin, providing varied terrain and open views. Local mountain bikers also love the Multitrampas – Camping Iratxe loop from Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Irache, a 12.8 miles (20.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Aberin is defined by valley hillsides, river paths, and varied natural features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Aberin are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aberin's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Aberin?
A: Aberin offers a substantial network of nearly 170 mountain bike trails. This includes a diverse range of options, with 18 easy routes, 114 moderate routes, and 36 difficult trails, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain and features can I expect on mountain bike trails in Aberin?
A: Mountain biking around Aberin features a diverse landscape of valley hillsides and varied terrain, offering a mix of ascents and descents. You'll find paths along the Río Ega, routes near features like the Muro de Aberin, and trails traversing natural landscapes from riverbanks to higher ground. The region provides varied terrain and open views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Aberin?
A: Yes, many routes in Aberin are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988518248/morentin-dicastillo-por-el-rio-ega-variante-circular-corta-a-la-integral-a-montejurra-mb7-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morentin-Dicastillo por el río Ega – variante circular corta a la Integral a Montejurra (MB7 BTT)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15003941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muro de Aberin. Robert Innova – Saint John the Baptist loop from Villatuerta&lt;/a&gt;, which also forms a loop.

Q: What are some family-friendly mountain bike trails in Aberin?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the 18 easy routes available in Aberin are generally suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These trails often feature gentler gradients and smoother surfaces, making them more accessible for riders of all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Aberin?
A: Many natural trails in Aberin are suitable for dogs, especially those that follow riverbanks or traverse open rural landscapes. However, it's always best to check local regulations or specific trail signage for any restrictions, and ensure your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash for their safety and that of other trail users.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking in Aberin?
A: Around Aberin, you can encounter several interesting landmarks and natural features. Some routes pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/382711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irache Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt;. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/718255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine Fountain — Bodegas Irache&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7387615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iglesia de San Pedro de la Rúa&lt;/a&gt;. For natural viewpoints, trails can lead to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cipriano&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Aberin?
A: Yes, many trails in Aberin offer scenic viewpoints, particularly those that ascend the valley hillsides. Routes around the Muro de Aberin provide open views, and trails leading towards summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cipriano&lt;/a&gt; will reward you with expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Aberin?
A: The mountain bike trails in Aberin are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscape, the mix of ascents and descents, and the scenic views along the Río Ega. The variety of routes, from riverside paths to higher ground, is also a common highlight.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Aberin?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trailhead, routes often start from towns or villages like Villatuerta or Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Irache, where public parking is typically available. It is advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking information.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Aberin?
A: Aberin's climate generally allows for mountain biking through much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and potentially some wet conditions, especially on lower trails.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails in Aberin?
A: Given that many routes start or pass through villages and towns such as Villatuerta, Morentin, or near the Monasterio de Santa María la Real de Irache, you can often find local cafes, bars, or pubs to refuel and relax after your ride. These establishments are typically located within or on the outskirts of these settlements.
tips: Short and fun singletrack but with a lot of history within the MTB Navarro. Different races of both the Open DN and the CR Cup have passed both up and down.
Very picturesque church with a typical Roman architecture of the Navarrese interior. It is also worth going up here to appreciate the wonderful panoramic views of the entire valley.
